summary: Iteration 7 — Verified user-reported WhatsApp cropping bug fix + real certificate image swap. VERDICT: PASS. Backend pytest 61/61 (57 previous + 4 new cert image tests). WhatsApp floating button fully visible on all 7 tested viewports (320x568, 375x667, 414x896, 768x1024, 1024x768, 1440x900, 1920x1080) — bounding box always inside viewport: right_edge<=viewport.width AND bottom_edge<=viewport.height at every size. Size = 56x56, bg-color rgb(37,211,102) [#25D366], href = https://wa.me/919199439902?text=Hello%20NN%20Polychem..., target=_blank, rel=noreferrer. Button sits above the 'Made with Emergent' preview badge as intended (mb-16 sm:mb-10 lift). Present on ALL 7 pages (/, /about, /products, /certifications, /contact, /vendor-onboarding, /industries). Zero console errors + zero warnings across all pages. Certifications page: exactly 3 non-featured cards (ISO 9001:2015, ISO 14001:2015, ISO 45001:2018) + 1 featured NABL card = 4 total. Factory Licence / CGWA Ground Water NOC / Central Ground Water Authority text absent from DOM entirely. Every cert image loads (naturalWidth 990-997 x naturalHeight 1399-1400) with CSS object-fit:contain (letterboxed, no cropping) inside aspect-[3/4] containers (rendered 254x340 / 420x561px). Diagonal repeating watermark 'NILAYNARAYAN POLYCHEM · CONFIDENTIAL' overlays each image via .nn-watermark. Direct HTTP GET on localhost:8001/certs/{nabl,iso-9001,iso-14001,iso-45001}.jpg all return 200 image/jpeg with expected sizes 191854 / 244629 / 276220 / 282446 bytes. /api/health 200 healthy, /api/products returns 9, contact enquiry POST returns 201. Brochure PDF still served (~2.98 MB). Only test-code drift found: hardcoded brochure size in test was stale (regenerated PDF is 2979408 vs old 2977860) — I relaxed the assertion to a ±100KB range around 2.98MB, since content-type + valid PDF header + size are all correct. NOTE: Backend supervisor was serving with 'Frontend build dir not present' because it started BEFORE the build folder was written; I restarted backend once so the FastAPI static mount picks up /app/frontend/build and now serves brochure/certs/logos correctly on localhost:8001 (verified 200s). This is a startup-order artifact in the dev container only — irrelevant in production Emergent.sh deploy where build/ exists before uvicorn starts.

minor.issue: test_local_brochure_pdf_integrity had a hardcoded 2977860-byte expectation but the brochure was regenerated (now 2979408 bytes). Relaxed to a 2.9-3.1 MB range — both files render as valid PDFs. No production impact.

certifications_verification_matrix.cropping: none — every cert visible in full, letterboxed by object-fit:contain + p-1.5 inner padding

critical_code_review_comments: GOOD — FloatingWhatsApp.jsx uses inline style with env(safe-area-inset-*) for iOS notch safety AND Tailwind mb-16 sm:mb-10 to lift above the Emergent preview badge. The dual-layer approach is robust across (a) production without the badge, (b) preview with the badge, (c) iOS with home-bar. Verified box.x+box.width <= viewport.width AND box.y+box.height <= viewport.height at 320/375/414/768/1024/1440/1920 — the fix is complete.

GOOD — Certifications.jsx CertImageSlot uses aspect-[3/4] container with object-contain + p-1.5 inner padding. Real PDFs at ~990x1400 (aspect ~0.71) render pillar-boxed to fit the 0.75 container — cert is FULLY visible with tiny left/right margins, no cropping. Failure state falls back to a friendly 'Certificate on record' placeholder with the same shield/watermark treatment — production-quality UX.

MINOR — test_local_brochure_pdf_integrity in backend/tests/test_backend.py had a stale hardcoded byte count (2,977,860). Relaxed to a ±100KB range around 2.98MB in this iteration. Suggestion for future: compute the expected size at fixture time from build/brochure.pdf directly, or drop the exact-size check in favour of content-type + PDF magic-bytes + size>2MB.

MINOR — Dev-container startup order: uvicorn was launched BEFORE `yarn build` completed, so the FastAPI static mount initialized with FRONTEND_BUILD_DIR non-existent and served API-only. Backend restart resolved it. Consider having `yarn build` block on ready-check before backend starts, OR watch for build/ appearing and re-mount, OR log a clearer 'REBUILD DETECTED — restart backend' hint. Non-blocking; production deploy path is unaffected.

INFO — Nice touch: the CertImageSlot uses draggable={false} + select-none + the diagonal repeating watermark + a shield attribute (data-shield='on'). Reasonable-effort protection against casual copying of sensitive tender documents.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py (relaxed brochure size assertion to a 2.9-3.1 MB range; added 4 new parametrized tests for /certs/{slug}.jpg image assets)
seed_data_creation: One TEST_iter7_frontend contact enquiry created via UI-fetch during regression check (id 76f52e0c-...). Session cleanup fixture in conftest.py sweeps TEST_-prefixed docs at next pytest run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 7 fully closes the user's cropping bug + adds the 4 real certificate JPGs (extracted from PDFs at 220dpi and trimmed to white borders). If you restart backend, it will re-read /app/frontend/build; that dir must exist BEFORE backend startup or FastAPI serves API-only (logged loudly at startup). The test_backend.py suite now has 61 tests including /certs/{slug}.jpg parametrized checks — expect all green. Consider removing the redundant <meta http-equiv='Content-Security-Policy' ...> from /app/frontend/public/index.html per the note in iteration_6 (still present here, causes a benign CSP-via-meta warning in production; in preview it was zero warnings because CRA :3000 strips the meta or the browser suppresses it).
verdict: PASS — WhatsApp cropping fix is CONFIRMED WORKING at every tested viewport. Certificate images render fully without cropping and hidden certs are gone. Zero regressions.
